strategic_objectives: retain freedom of action in airspace and on the northern front
avoid strategic isolation while preserving rapid decision cycles

strengths: deep integration of warning and strike systems
preferred_instruments: tight coordination with US resupply and messaging
readiness_drivers: sortie generation from central and northern belts

decision_doctrine.risk_tolerance: high under immediate threat, moderate for prolonged regional war
decision_doctrine.escalation_bias: preemptive when warning quality is strong, otherwise retaliatory but concentrated
decision_doctrine.negotiation_posture: accepts pauses that restore deterrence without locking in vulnerability
red_lines: mass-casualty saturation of major urban centers
loss of freedom of action over northern airspace
major damage to strategic energy or command infrastructure
persistent precision-guided fire from Lebanon or Syria
off_ramps: restored civil aviation and port confidence

economic_pressure_points: port slowdowns in Haifa and Ashdod
intelligence_priorities: proxy movement in Lebanon and Syria
civil-defense strain across the northern and coastal belts

priority_fronts.name: Northern Border
priority_fronts.goal: prevent sustained launch saturation from south Lebanon

priority_fronts.name: Syrian Transfer Corridor
priority_fronts.goal: interrupt precision weapons movement and proxy reinforcement

priority_fronts.name: Central Home Front
priority_fronts.goal: keep command continuity, airports, and economic core functioning

priority_fronts.name: Eastern Mediterranean
priority_fronts.goal: preserve naval depth, gas-field continuity, and reinforcement routes

strategic_relationships.dependency: resupply, political cover, and shared deterrent signaling
strategic_relationships.notes: Most important external stabilizer and escalation amplifier.
Quiet cooperation matters more during multi-front stress.
Valuable for regional containment and maritime confidence.

strategic_relationships.dependency: mutual deterrence logic through pain thresholds
strategic_relationships.notes: Force survivability and launch tempo are core readouts.

strategic_relationships.dependency: indirect signaling through proxies and strategic messaging
strategic_relationships.notes: Israel reads Tehran largely through corridor activity and launch quality.

geopolitical_chokepoints.name: Blue Line / south Lebanon approaches
geopolitical_chokepoints.desired_state: launch pressure kept below strategic saturation

geopolitical_chokepoints.name: Ben Gurion air corridor
geopolitical_chokepoints.desired_state: usable for civil and military continuity

geopolitical_chokepoints.name: Haifa-Ashdod logistics coast
geopolitical_chokepoints.desired_state: ports and coastal industry remain operational

geopolitical_chokepoints.name: Eastern Mediterranean gas and naval belt
geopolitical_chokepoints.desired_state: stable enough to preserve economic and naval depth

geospatial_focus: Tel Aviv-Jerusalem decision and population core
Haifa and the coastal industrial belt
